rationale: The citing paper uses Aaker and Lee (2006) in its theoretical development of regulatory compatibility effects, specifically when arguing that message cues matching regulatory orientation increase persuasion and motivation. That logic is directly tied to the paper's hypotheses about coupon framing and expiration restrictions affecting basket outcomes. The citation is also used again in discussion to interpret observed compatibility effects, indicating an ongoing conceptual role rather than a one-off background mention.
relation_description: The citing paper treats the cited paper as part of the conceptual foundation for regulatory fit/regulatory relevance, then tests those compatibility ideas in a sales-promotion and shopping-basket context. It builds on that fit framework to explain how promotion cues can shape broader basket size and composition.
